question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 9.00% on 2026-01-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the Democrats control the House of Representatives at any point before Jan 3, 2027?"?
summary: Meta-prediction about whether the Metaculus CP for "Democrats control House before Jan 3, 2027" will be above 9% on Jan 14, 2026. The underlying event is very unlikely (2-5% actual probability): Democrats need a net 7-seat swing from 213D/219R with zero party flips in special elections so far. The initial CP was ~60% in Feb 2025 when the gap was only 3 seats. The critical unknown is whether the CP has dropped 85%+ from its initial value to below 9%. With only 22 forecasters and Metaculus's sticky aggregation, such a dramatic drop is possible but somewhat unlikely. Stale high forecasts likely keep the CP elevated above 9%, but there's genuine uncertainty since I couldn't observe the current CP.

factors.description: Initial CP was ~60% - enormous buffer above the 9% threshold. An 85%+ drop from initial value is needed for NO resolution.
Only 22 forecasters on underlying question - small pools tend to have stickier, slower-adjusting CPs due to less frequent updating.
Gap widened from 3 to 6 seats over 11 months; zero party flips in special elections. Strong evidence the underlying probability is very low (2-5%).
Metaculus recency weighting could heavily discount old 60% forecasts if most active forecasters have updated to 3-8%.
Path to Democratic control requires unprecedented net 7-seat swing through special elections/resignations only. Informed forecasters would be very pessimistic.
Some stale forecasts from inactive users likely persist at 20-60%, pulling the weighted median upward even if active forecasters are at 3-8%.

reasoning:

Let me get the CP history.The CP history data seems limited. Let me get more recent data and check the current political situation.Let me search for more recent information about the House composition and any special elections.This is very helpful. Let me get the current CP directly from the question page.Now let me synthesize everything I know and run the final analysis.**Forecast: 55% probability** that the CP will be above 9% on January 14, 2026.

**Key reasoning:**

- **Underlying event is very unlikely (~2-5%)**: Democrats need a net 7-seat swing from 213D/219R, achievable only through special elections and resignations. Zero party flips have occurred in this Congress.
- **Initial CP was ~60%** (Feb 2025, when the gap was only 3 seats). The gap has since widened to 6 seats.
- **The critical unknown**: Has the CP dropped 85%+ from its initial value? This is the decisive question I couldn't directly answer because the CP history API only returned data from the question's first day.
- **FOR CP > 9%**: Small forecaster pool (22), Metaculus CP stickiness, stale high forecasts from inactive users pulling the aggregate up.
- **AGAINST CP > 9%**: Fundamentals strongly point to <5% probability, recency weighting could discount old forecasts heavily, informed forecasters would be at 3-8%.

The 55% reflects genuine uncertainty about where the CP currently sits, not hedging. If I could see the actual current CP, my confidence would shift dramatically in either direction.
